post.description: One-shot endpoint for installing the first admin in a fresh deployment. Idempotent: returns 409 once any admin exists. Disabled unless ``SEEDBANK_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN`` is configured; the request must present the matching token.

get.description:
Aggregated performance for one model.

Two sources merged into one envelope:

* ``offline_metrics`` — Phase 7 ``model_metrics`` rows written by the
  experiment runner (always available).
* ``rows`` — Phase 8 ClickHouse ``fact_inference`` aggregates
  (returns empty + a degradation ``note`` until that table exists).

patch.description: Atomically replace the traffic splits for one segment.

post.description:
Upload one or more images. The server stores them in MinIO, creates a `scan_batch` (status=`pending`), and dispatches Celery tasks. The response is HTTP 202 with the batch envelope; clients poll `GET /api/v1/batches/{id}` for results.

The `model_id` override is restricted to `ai_developer` and `admin`.

delete.description:
Soft-delete one batch the caller owns (admins: any).

404 if it doesn't exist, isn't owned, or is already deleted — same
non-enumeration rule as the read endpoints.

get.description:
Short-lived presigned URLs for the batch's images.

The client pairs these with the normalized bounding boxes from
``GET /batches/{id}`` to render detections over the original image —
the bytes are served straight from object storage, never proxied
through the API.

post.description:
Soft-delete up to 200 owned batches in one call.

Best-effort: IDs the caller doesn't own or that are already deleted are
skipped, and ``deleted`` reports how many actually took effect. Returns 200
(not 204) because the count is the useful part of the response.

get.description:
Download every detection in the batch as a CSV file.

Columns are fixed (:data:`_EXPORT_COLUMNS`) and rows are ordered by image
then detection id, so re-exports diff cleanly. Decimals are emitted via the
schema so they match the JSON wire format (``"0.9234"``, not a lossy float).

200.description: Every detection in the batch as CSV.

get.description:
Download every detection in the batch as JSON.

Same data and ordering as the CSV export, wrapped in the standard
``Envelope`` so it's consistent with the rest of the API. The
``Content-Disposition`` header nudges browsers to save it as a file.

Probes DB, Redis, MinIO, and ClickHouse. Returns degraded statuses
per-component so the operator can tell what's broken from one ping.

Production-model readiness is added in Phase 5 once the model
manager exists.

ApiKeyOut.title: ApiKeyOut
ApiKeyOut.description:
Returned on **creation only**. The plaintext `key` is included exactly
once and never retrievable again.

BatchBulkDeleteIn.title: BatchBulkDeleteIn
BatchBulkDeleteIn.description:
Request body for ``POST /batches/delete`` — bulk soft-delete.

Capped so a single call can't fan out an unbounded ``IN (...)``. IDs the
caller doesn't own (or that are already deleted) are silently skipped; the
response reports how many actually took effect.

BatchDeleteResult.title: BatchDeleteResult
BatchDeleteResult.description: How many batches a (bulk) delete actually soft-deleted.

BatchDetailOut.title: BatchDetailOut
BatchDetailOut.description:
Full nested envelope for ``GET /batches/{id}`` — batch → images
→ inferences → detections, eager-loaded by the repository.

BatchOut.title: BatchOut
BatchOut.description:
Lightweight batch summary — the ``POST /analyze`` response and
each row of the ``GET /batches`` page.

``image_count`` is populated by the service layer (a count query or
``len(files)``); it is *not* a column on ``scan_batches``.

BootstrapAdminIn.title: BootstrapAdminIn
BootstrapAdminIn.description:
Payload for ``POST /auth/bootstrap-admin``.

The ``bootstrap_token`` field is a shared secret rotated out of the
environment after first-admin creation; the password rules match
``RegisterIn`` so the resulting admin can immediately log in.

DatasetItemCreateIn.title: DatasetItemCreateIn
DatasetItemCreateIn.description:
One item inside a bulk-add request.

``image_storage_key`` references an object the caller has already
uploaded to MinIO ``seedbank-datasets`` (presigned PUT or
``scripts/upload_dataset.py``). The server does not fetch or
validate the bytes synchronously — the experiment runner does that
when it dereferences the item.

``ground_truth`` is a free-form JSON document; the experiment runner
enforces the schema (``{"kind": "detection", "boxes": [...]}`` or
``{"kind": "classification", "label": "good"}``).

DatasetItemsAddedOut.title: DatasetItemsAddedOut
DatasetItemsAddedOut.description: Response body for the bulk-add endpoint.

ExperimentCreateIn.title: ExperimentCreateIn
ExperimentCreateIn.description:
Request body for ``POST /experiments``.

``name`` is human-readable; ``model_id`` and ``dataset_id`` are the
only required references. The worker resolves both during dispatch.

ExperimentDetailOut.title: ExperimentDetailOut
ExperimentDetailOut.description: Detail view — same as summary plus the embedded result count.

ExperimentSummaryOut.title: ExperimentSummaryOut
ExperimentSummaryOut.description: List-row + create-response shape — small enough to ship in lists.

ImageUrlOut.title: ImageUrlOut
ImageUrlOut.description:
A short-lived, browser-reachable URL for one stored scan image.

Returned by ``GET /batches/{id}/image-urls`` so the client can render the
original image (and overlay normalized bounding boxes) without the bytes
ever traversing the API process. ``expires_at`` lets the client refetch
before the presigned URL lapses.

ModelPerformanceOut.title: ModelPerformanceOut
ModelPerformanceOut.description:
Aggregated performance for one model.

``offline_metrics`` come from Postgres ``model_metrics`` (Phase 7
experiment runner upserts them). ``rows`` come from ClickHouse fact
tables (Phase 8); until that table exists the field is empty and
``note`` carries a human-readable degradation reason.

OfflineMetricOut.title: OfflineMetricOut
OfflineMetricOut.description: One row from ``model_metrics`` — an offline-eval summary metric.

SeedTypeOut.title: SeedTypeOut
SeedTypeOut.description: List entry for a :class:`SeedType` — the catalog dropdown source.

SupplierUpdateIn.title: SupplierUpdateIn
SupplierUpdateIn.description: Request body for ``PATCH /suppliers/{id}`` — all fields optional.

TrafficSplitReplaceIn.title: TrafficSplitReplaceIn
TrafficSplitReplaceIn.description: Atomic replacement of all active splits for ``(kind, seed_type_id)``.
